response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and backend engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and web-platform credentials, but he does not meet the core requirements for this role. Most critically, there is no mention of Japanese language proficiency anywhere in the resume, which is a hard non-negotiable requirement. Beyond that disqualifying gap, his experience in C programming is minimal and not oriented toward embedded firmware or UNIX systems programming, he has no demonstrated background in network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), and there is no evidence of Linux kernel-level development or LAN-switch / embedded networking work.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, backend, and some embedded/hardware contexts, demonstrating adaptability
Demonstrated C and C++ usage at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and in a Linux-based audio synthesis thesis project
Familiarity with Linux environments and low-latency systems programming (e.g., async I/O, fibers at Sociomantic)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency stated — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with embedded networking security features (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chip SDKs, or BIOS/bootloader)
C programming appears only in peripheral roles; primary expertise is TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js, which is entirely misaligned with the embedded firmware C focus

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security feature experience
General software engineering depth and longevity
